entry.title: Re: Numeric Point
entry.content:
Hi Lothar

Yeah I was testing this out, but I don't like this - requires I have a dimension with only 1 value for all the data sources.

Also it means I will have that value standing in the top left of the widget 😞 Can't hide it.

But thanks

 

BR

Nick

entry.title: Assembly order process - WBS structure
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working on a change request for the assembly order process. I've completed all the necessary configurations and set up the standard template for the WBS structure. Upon saving the sales order, the project is being created successfully.

However, the issue I'm facing is that only the project definition, level-one WBS, and the network header are being generated, and the WBS structure up to level 3 is not being created.

Since we are not using activities, I haven’t allocated any WBS elements to activities in the standard network. I suspect this might be the reason for the incomplete WBS structure.

Could anyone advise on generating the full WBS structure in the assembly order process without using network activities?

Thanks 

Praveen

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • Women
    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
    • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I’m thrilled about this opportunity and eager to contribute. While I’m new to SAP, I’ve worked with other accounting systems and am confident in my ability to learn and excel. My goal is to become a skilled SAP consultant and join the professional community. I’m excited to start my training journey and see where it takes me. Please send the the registration link. 

Thank you so much!

entry.title: SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM - Need to be run on Solution Manager?
entry.content:
Good Day

Can you run ALM readiness check on an ECC system without Solution Manager? Basically do you need Solution Manager to run ALM readiness checks?

Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM - Need to be ru...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM supports the analysis of SAP Solution Manager 7.2 systems.

If you are either considering or planning to move your SAP Solution Manager 7.2 system completely or partially to SAP Cloud ALM. As a result, you can analyze your existing SAP Solution Manager system with the SAP Readiness Check tool for SAP Cloud ALM, to help scope and plan your project.

It does not make sense to run it on an ECC system. 

see ##3236443 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP Cloud ALM

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Build process automation

entry.title: Re: Deploying App into SAP Build Work Zone / Fiori Launchpad in BTP
entry.content: wonderful blog, thanks for sharing excellent content..! @Hari89

entry.title: Re: Deploying App into SAP Build Work Zone / Fiori Launchpad in BTP
entry.content: Thanks for sharing @Hari89 ! Nice blog

entry.title: Re: Details of Write/Read Options and Value - Calling LDA...
entry.content:
Hi 

Please find the details for `LDAP_OPT_RESTART` and `LDAP_OPT_ERROR_NUMBER`:

LDAP_OPT_RESTART (Option Number: 9)
Meaning: This option controls whether the LDAP client should automatically retry an operation after a transient failure, such as a network timeout.
Functionality:
If enabled, the client attempts to reconnect and retry the operation if it encounters specific errors (like network interruptions).
This is useful for improving application resilience in environments with unstable connections.
Recommended Values:
`0`: Disable auto-restart (default behavior).
`1`: Enable auto-restart.

LDAP_OPT_ERROR_NUMBER (Option Number: 49)
Meaning: This option allows the client to retrieve the last error code that occurred during an LDAP operation.
Functionality:
After performing an LDAP operation (e.g., search, bind), you can check this option to get the integer error code representing the type of error encountered.
This is particularly useful for debugging and handling specific error scenarios in your application logic.
Usage: Typically, you call the function to retrieve the error number after an operation fails, and it does not have a setting value since it is read-only.

entry.title: Re: Consuming Data from Datasphere to Azure Data Factory via ODBC
entry.content:
Hello @vignesh3027 

Thanks a lot for the detailed and very helpful Blog 😉

As you might guess... there is one question remaining regarding the setup...

Imagine, the data you would like to transfer is the same, which is used for reporting purposes in SAC. That means you have a Fact view which protects the data via Data Access Controls.

How can you enable the database user to read "all" data from the view (or the analytic model, if you use this as source)? Is there a connection you can create from the database user to the user tables, created for DAC (which is usually the e-mail address)?

Thanks, Martin

entry.title: Re: How to register delivery dates for supplier responses...
entry.content: Better than this please use the PO API directly to update delivery dates which is the 3rd option of my reply which will also provide you the same functionality. I am not sure whether this app is of help related to Data Exchange exist or not, please raise OSS with SAP for that.

entry.title: Hi I need sample fs document for sap mm buniness scnerio.
Re: HU and Packaging Material Type in EWM S/4
entry.content: YEs , Packaging material type will always be 1 to 1 mapped with HU Type

entry.title: Re: COFC Period Posting issue for Work Order
entry.content:
Hello Preethi,
since it is not possible to post to a blocked period, you must specify the posting date for these periods in CAT9. Here you enter a value from the currently opened period as the posting date or as the cancellation date. /See picture)
Best regards
Marcus

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
@David_Chaviano I have completed Exploring SAP Cloud ERP. I have strong interest in SAP. I I consider it as penetrating in all walks of life in future and expect that huge number of industries will adopt and implement SAP. In this regard the certified SAP resources in various regions will become assets and valuable resources so I am interested to get certified in at least 03 areas in SAP as I have previous experience in various areas project management, Cloud implementation, Procurement and HR. I want to get maximum benefit from SAP generous offer of free attempt and want to learn maximum about SAP and to become expert in various SAP modules. 

Never stop learning, you stop learning and your progress and prosperity stops.

Thankyou SAP for providing this opportunity

I'm trying to send a notification to the approver once the downpayment is done for the sales order in S4HANA Public Cloud . How can I do this? Please Help me with this .

Thanks in Advance

Shraddha

entry.title: SAP EWM Stuck Queue - QM processing currently only possible for integrated warehouses
entry.content:
Hi Experts, we need your help here Once we goods receipt the quality management enabled products in warehouse the Inspection lot will be created. In mean time we are facing the Stuck queue called "QM processing currently only possible for integrated warehouses" this queue is carrying the Goods receipt info back to s4 , However, it is getting stuck and there is no goods movement completion was posted back in ERP.

May i know what the root cause for this would be?

EWM - Quality Management EWM - Interfaces EWM - Goods Movement

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas Camera IPad Auto focus
entry.content: We are using the iPad camera to take pictures of food products during a quality control process in QM. When taking a photo with the iPad's internal camera application, you have to touch the screen to trigger auto focus if no face is detected. In Slipstrean Engine, the camera application for creating an attachment doesn't trigger the auto focus and the photo is blurred. What should I do ?

entry.title: Re: Goods Movement Types
entry.content:
Hello @Wenny 

In cloud there is access for OMJJ transaction, hence there is no central place to check for movement types, however refer to below of my old note which talks about other SSCUIs which can be referred to check for movement types.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002865146 

Best Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: Fuzzy search for Business Partners in SAP S/4HANA on premise without TREX?
entry.content:
I have looked into SAP Fiori Apps and found the answer to my own question. The Fiori App "Manage Business Partner Master Data" allows doing a fuzzy search and fulfills the business requirement.
Link to Fiori Apps Library: SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (ondemand.com)

entry.title: #Coe#BUMA Custom Logic BAdI / Extensibility for Service Performer in Purchase Requisition
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a BAdI or custom logic extensibility for Service Performer in Purchase Requisition? Currently, Service Performer is only available in MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ITEM for Purchase Order.

Thank you,

Jen

entry.title: Re: “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
entry.content:
Hello @FreekKeijzer 

very interesting Blog... had fun while reading it. 😉

So far, I also had one more item on your list:

5. Number of reporting users

Don't you think it matters (at least in bigger companies), if you have ~100 or more users who are using reports in parallel at the beginning or end of the month, and affect the system, CPU, processes? There is the well-known "Whitepaper" of SAP regarding embedded BW, with max 20% of S4 data volume... but it affects CPU and processes...

However, regarding eBW. As long there is no end of life of it, in e.g. S4 HANA 2030, it might also one option to use this to include small amounts of external data.

I think you mainly focused on S4 tables only, but what do you think of the eBW capabilities in terms of embedded analytics?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Query manager yellow arrow for docnum from QIPF
entry.content:
Hi,

The query is ok but you need to check document numbering for Landed Cost. You may defined same document number for multi period (restart numbering).

For example:

2017: 1 >>> 9999

2024: 1 >>> 9999

In this case, It is better to use Internal Number instead of Document Number for Drill Down.

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: Setup Approval Process for Sales Order in S4Hana Cloud
entry.content:
Hi community and all experts,

I would like to seek some help in setting up the approval process for Sales Order with a Net Value Condition. I've tried but the approval process doesn't kick in when the order reached or exceed that limit.

This is what I've setup so far in the system.

1st, i define the approval request in the configurations.

2nd, i setup the workflow for sales order with a net amount condition.

3rd, i created a custom logic for the workflow.

I've also checked to make sure the workflow scenario is activated.

However, my test Sales Order didn't trigger the approval process that I've setup.

 

May i ask what have i missed or could it be my setup is wrong?

Thank you very much for all guidance.

Best Regards,

Dan

entry.title: eDelivery Cancellation Turkey - Status
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We need your help for the eDelivery integration for Turkey.

We are receiving a "Not Found" response code from our provider and are trying to map it to "Cancelled" in SAP CPI. Within the "Cancelled" status, the eDelivery can be cancelled in the eDocument cockpit and the GI reversed.  But we haven’t been successful in implementing this mapping. Does anyone have experience with remapping status codes in CPI? Any guidance or tips would be appreciated.

Thank you very much and best regards,

Annika

entry.title: Create multiple database users in Datasphere spaces
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I need to create multiple database users in space management (database user related to a space).

The request is to have a user mapping from a Front end BI Tool so I can use datasphere DAC to manage row securities.

The editor lets you create one user at time,

And the bad part is that passwords are auto-generated, most of the time with characters that are not accepted by the front end tool.
I need to create around 350 users.

Does some sort of SAML / SSO exists for database users?
As far as I know Datasphere's database is a "self-managed" hana cloud so we can not create users from there and assign correct roles to see space data.

Do you have any hint?

Thanks,

Alberto

entry.title: Internal Pricing Error after using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am creating a goods movement whenever changes in the SD meet certain conditions using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE in user exit MV45AFZZ - USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE.

I am not passing any price or pricing conditions to BAPI. However, I am getting an popup message STOP - Internal Pricing Error.

This could be because the PRICING_REFRESH function module has been called up in the BAPI, even though you did not require it.

Please provide your valuable inputs.

Best Regards,

Nicole

Dorina

Since I am not using sap mobile app,
I would like to launch the mobile browser directly without displaying this screen.
Has anyone else experience this? Thank you!

entry.title: How to Create a Certificate as a PDF and Send it as a Mail Attachment from SAP CPI
entry.content:
Introduction:

In this blog post, we will walk through how to generate a PDF certificate using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) and send it as an email attachment. This approach can be used for various applications, including generating certificates of completion, awards, or any other document requiring dynamic content. We will be using Groovy scripts to dynamically create PDFs with personalized content.

Prerequisites:

  • Access to SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration).
  • Basic knowledge of Groovy scripting.
  • Basic understanding of HTML and PDF generation libraries (like PDFBox or Flying Saucer).
  • Mail sender configuration in SAP CPI.

Step 1: Setting Up the Integration Flow

  1. Create an Integration Flow in SAP CPI.
  2. Add the necessary artifacts, such as Groovy scripts, to handle the PDF generation and email sending.

I have created a normal iFlow just for testing, as this blog is on how to create certificate as a PDF so I assume you are having an idea on how to create an iFlow.

In my Iflow I have added a Content Modifier which is containing many different fields as properties which I am going to use in my Groovy Script as well as in Mail adapter.

Step 2: Creating the Groovy Script to Generate the PDF

You will need to use Groovy to:

  • Generate dynamic content (such as name, date, and other certificate details).
  • Convert the HTML content into a PDF.

Before writing a Groovy Scripts you need to include specific external libraries because in SAP CPI, Groovy scripts don’t natively support PDF generation or manipulation or doesn't have built-in functionality for HTML-to-PDF conversion and many more reasons which you can learn on internet why do you have to use the following libraries.

1. flying-saucer-core-9.1.20

2. flying-saucer-core-9.1.20-javadoc

3. flying-saucer-core-9.1.20-sources

4. flying-saucer-pdf-9.1.20

5. fontbox-2.0.32

6. itext-2.1.7

7. pdfbox-2.0.32

8. pdfbox-io-3.0.3

After adding all the libraries, you cann work on writing a Groovy Script. 

I am attaching a sample groovy script which I have used to creating a normal certificate, which you can edit as per your requirements.

 

 

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import org.xhtmlrenderer.pdf.ITextRenderer
import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream
import javax.mail.util.ByteArrayDataSource
import org.apache.camel.impl.DefaultAttachment

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Fetch properties from the message
    String fullName = message.getProperty("FullName")
    String completionDate = message.getProperty("Date")
    String base64Image = message.getProperty("Image")

    // HTML structure for the certificate with dynamic content and embedded image at the top
    String certificateHTML = """
        <html>
        <head>
            <style>
                @font-face {
                    font-family: 'Noto Sans Hebrew';
                    src: url('data:font/woff2;base64,YOUR_BASE64_FONT_DATA') format('woff2');
                    font-weight: normal;
                    font-style: normal;
                }
                body {
                    font-family: 'Noto Sans Hebrew', sans-serif;
                    direction: ltr; /* Left-to-right for English */
                }
                .container {
                    width: 80%;
                    margin: 0 auto;
                    border: 1px solid #e1e1e1;
                    padding: 20px;
                    text-align: center;
                }
                .header {
                    font-size: 18px;
                    font-weight: bold;
                    margin-bottom: 10px;
                }
                .content {
                    text-align: left;
                    font-size: 16px;
                }
                .signature {
                    margin-top: 50px;
                    text-align: left;
                }
                .date {
                    text-align: right;
                    margin-top: 30px;
                    font-size: 14px;
                }
                .footer {
                    margin-top: 20px;
                    font-size: 12px;
                    color: #808080;
                    text-align: center;
                }
                .image-container {
                    margin-bottom: 20px; /* Add space below the image */
                    text-align: center;
                }
            </style>
        </head>
        <body>
            <div class="container">
                <div class="image-container">
                    <img src="data:image/png;base64,${base64Image}" alt="Certificate Logo" width="100" height="100" />
                </div>
                <div class="header">
                    Certificate of Completion
                </div>
                <div class="content">
                    This is to certify that
                    <br /><br />
                    <b>${fullName}</b>
                    <br />
                    has successfully completed the following training:
                    <br />
                    Decision on Granting Ardalin
                </div>
                <div class="date">
                    Training completed on ${completionDate}
                </div>
                <div class="signature">
                    Approved by System Admin
                </div>
                <div class="footer">
                    Generated by System Admin
                </div>
            </div>
        </body>
        </html>
    """

    // Convert HTML to PDF using Flying Saucer with iText
    ByteArrayOutputStream pdfOutputStream = new ByteArrayOutputStream()
    ITextRenderer renderer = new ITextRenderer()

    // Set the HTML content to the renderer
    renderer.setDocumentFromString(certificateHTML)
    renderer.layout()

    // Generate the PDF
    renderer.createPDF(pdfOutputStream)

    // Close the stream
    pdfOutputStream.close()

    // Create attachment for email
    def dataSource = new ByteArrayDataSource(pdfOutputStream.toByteArray(), "application/pdf")
    def attachment = new DefaultAttachment(dataSource)
    message.addAttachmentObject("certificate.pdf", attachment)

    return message
}

 

 

 

 

After adding the Script in your process, configure your mail adapter.

I am only showing the last part of it as I assume you know how to configure Mail Adapter.

Step 3: Testing and Deploying

  • Test the integration flow by triggering the process and verifying that the PDF certificate is correctly attached to the email.
  • Deploy the Integration Flow in SAP CPI.

In my case I have received a Mail and a certifacte like given in below images.

Conclusion:

By following these steps, you can generate a personalized certificate in PDF format and send it via email from SAP CPI. This technique can be expanded for various document generation and delivery use cases.

I hope you have learnt something new from this blog post. If you have any question, feel free to comment.

entry.title: Re: How to get data sent from Gmail in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check the below link.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/cloud-platform-integration-gmail-configuration-and-integration-flow/ba-p/13488863

I hope this is what you're looking for.

 

Regards,

Prithviraj

entry.title: Re: #Coe#BUMA Custom Logic BAdI / Extensibility for Servi...
entry.content:
Hi 

Please try the below steps:

Yes, for enhancing the Service Performer in Purchase Requisitions, you can use:

1. BAdI: ME_PROCESS_PR_CUST - Customize processing logic for Purchase Requisitions.
2. BAdI: ME_BESTBUY - Enhance service provider selection.
3. User Exits - Check relevant exits like `EXIT_SAPMM06E_005`.
4. Enhancement Spots - Look for spots related to Purchase Requisitions.
5. Fiori Customization - Adapt UI in Fiori apps if applicable.

entry.title: Re: Connection error when verifying Google API key
entry.content:
Hi Shane,

Thanks, but that's the default screen that I also have and the key appears shorter.

However, if I you un-tick the Windows TTS box, i.e. make sure that you are really using the Google one, is TTS still working with this short key? 

Thanks a lot!

Regards,

Roumiana

entry.title: Re: Audit trail to track old and new values on table, HAN...
entry.content:
Hi 

Please check the below To track old and new values in HANA DB for audit trails, you can use:

1. Change Data Capture (CDC): Implement CDC to track changes on tables. HANA provides a built-in mechanism for this.

2. Audit Logging: Enable audit logging in HANA to capture DML operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) on tables.

3. Triggers: Create database triggers to log changes in custom audit tables whenever data is modified.

4. Temporal Tables: Use temporal tables to automatically track historical data changes, if applicable.

These methods will help you maintain an audit trail for standard DB tables.

entry.title: Re: #Coe#BUMA Custom Logic BAdI / Extensibility for Servi...
entry.content: Hi Umasaral, is the item 1 available in S/4 Hana Public Cloud?

entry.title: Re: How to access application with app/router
entry.content:
Hi

Please check this 

Correct Path: Ensure `path: app/router` points to the right folder with `xs-app.json`.

2. Check `xs-app.json`: Confirm it has correct routes configured.

3. Destinations: Verify that all required destinations (like `srv-api`) are properly set up and accessible.

4. Health Checks: Ensure readiness checks are configured for the app router.

5. Review Logs: Check application logs for specific errors during startup or access attempts.

Redeploy the MTA after verifying these points.

entry.title: Re: #Coe#BUMA Custom Logic BAdI / Extensibility for Servi...
entry.content:
Hi 

No, the BAdI `ME_PROCESS_PR_CUST` is not available in the S/4HANA Public Cloud environment. In this environment, you typically use the Extensibility Framework for customizations, which includes business configuration and managed service extensions rather than BAdIs. You can explore options like:

Custom Fields and Logic: Use this to add fields and implement simple logic.
Fiori Elements: Leverage UI adaptations.
Business Events: Use these for integrating custom logic.

For custom logic in the Public Cloud, you should focus on these extensibility options rather than traditional BAdIs.

entry.title: Re: INSTALLATION CRYSTAL REPORT
entry.content:
Hi,

May be you can have a look at the below documentation and see if that helps:

https://help.sap.com/doc/8f5d9411fa6f4b0aa9bc0e7fac33f059/10.0/en-US/How_to_Set_Up_Data_Sources_for_Crystal_Reports.pdf

https://help.sap.com/doc/f2afcd40db8a4c269616991680df6e04/10.0/en-US/How_to_Work_with_SAP_Crystal_Reports_in_SAP_Business_One.pdf

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: From CAD to Manufacturing as-a-Service. New Idea-to-Market Public Cloud Scenario available
entry.content: What a journey it has been! Love this next big step in serving SAP PLM to companies around the world!

entry.title: Re: Returns Warehouse can be used for invoices
entry.content:
Hi 

Please check the below steps To restrict user access to a returns warehouse for invoices in CCO FP 20 PL03, you should:

1. Adjust User Permissions: Modify the user roles to remove permissions for accessing the returns warehouse during invoice processes.

2. Set Warehouse Configuration: Ensure that the returns warehouse is configured only for credit memos in the stock location settings.

3. Check Document Type Settings: Verify the document type settings to ensure that the returns warehouse cannot be selected for invoice types.

4. Implement Validation Rules: Consider implementing validation rules in the system to block transactions that attempt to use the returns warehouse for invoices.

After making these changes, test the user access to ensure the restriction is effective.

entry.title: Re: “I now have SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. Do I still need a data warehouse?”
entry.content:
Operational Analytics vs DW Analytics is an old debate.

To analyze something like "amount of unpaid bills", you only need data from the operational system. So you don't need a DW.

To analyze things like "Why we have a higher amount of unpaid bills and how it's related to geographic, economic, social indicators" (that you don't have in the operational system), then yes, you need a DW.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Variance (Q/Q and Y/Y) in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Nagarajan,

you're facing a common issue with dynamic reporting where cell references for Q/Q and Y/Y variances do not automatically adjust as you move to future quarters. Here are some steps you can take to resolve this:

1. Use Relative References: Ensure that your formulas for Q/Q and Y/Y variances use relative references instead of absolute ones. This allows the formulas to adjust automatically when you shift quarters.

2. Dynamic Named Ranges: Consider using named ranges that dynamically adjust to the data range. This way, as you add new quarters, the references update accordingly.

3. OFFSET Function: Utilize the `OFFSET` function in your variance calculations to reference the correct cells based on the current quarter's position.

4. Check Data Table Structure: Make sure your data table is structured consistently, and there are no gaps that might disrupt cell referencing.

5. Testing with Sample Data: Create a small sample table to test your formulas and ensure they behave correctly as you move to different quarters.

By implementing these strategies, you should be able to overcome the issue of incorrect variances and ensure your report updates correctly for future quarters.

entry.title: Network Request Error: for Post Call from Build apps to Document information extraction service
entry.content:
I'm trying to Take Picture in Build apps and send image via POST Call to Document information Extraction service using JS workflow function.
1. as Destinations Does not support Multipart content as mentioned in the Issue with SAP BTP Destination Rest API Integratio... - SAP Community 

2. with reference to PDF Sending to REST API with JavaScript in SAP Bui... - SAP Community, Explore and Infuse DOX capabilities in SAP BTP cus... - SAP Community
I have written the Java script : 

try {
// Declare and initialize variables inside the try block
let url = inputs.url;
let file = inputs.file;
let Name = inputs.imageName;
let Token = inputs.Token;
let imPath = inputs.imagePath
// Fetch the image blob from the imagePath
let iPath = await fetch(imPath);
let blob = await iPath.blob();

// Prepare the form data
const formData = new FormData();
formData.append('file', blob, Name);

// Define the options for the request
var lv_options = {
"clientId": "default",
"documentType": "custom",
"schemaName": "Danfoss"
};
formData.append('options', JSON.stringify(lv_options));

// Make the POST request
const response = await fetch(url, {
method: 'POST',
headers: {
Accept: "application/json",
"X-Requested-With": "XMLHttpRequest",
Authorization: "Bearer " + Token
},
body: formData
});

if (response.ok) {
let data = await response.json();
return { id: data.id };
} else {
// Handle HTTP errors
alert("HTTP-Error: " + response.status);
}
} catch (error) {
// Handle errors that occur during fetch or JSON parsing
console.error("Error occurred:", error);
alert("An error occurred: " + error.message);
}

3. Also Tried the Approach of Pick files from Build apps and replicated as it is mentioned in  PDF Sending to REST API with JavaScript in SAP Bui... - SAP Community

But still i'm Getting the Network Request Failed error. i don't know where i'm going wrong
Can anyone Please help me with this.
Thanks in Advance.

@Dan_Wroblewski @EvgeniyGorbunov

entry.title: Re: Update OITM
entry.content:
Hello Makhamad,

An UPDATE command is not allowed. But your problem has nothing to do with the UPDATE command. The error message says that there is a syntax error in a formatted search. The query for the formatted search is probably called "Товары/услуги - ЕИ продаж по умолчанию".

Best regards

Inga

entry.title: Re: Doubts in pricing Secure Login Service for SAPGUI
entry.content:
Hi Carlos,

yes, that is correct.

Best regards,

Christian

entry.title: Re: Segmentation at company code level
entry.content:
The described business change / requirement is a very common one and known - especially in the automotive industry. Materials or production areas have to be moved into another segment to the EV segment.

Nevertheless having defined a segmentation on company code level is a customer specific setup. SAP recommends to use Segments or Profit Centers for segmentation and external segment reporting.

Based on this SAP recommended segment configuration the profit center reorganization has been developed, which supports an easy reassignment of profit centers to products/materials, to projects and many other master data and takes care of transfer posting the related inventory and other balance sheet items to the new profit center. A similar functionality is planned to be provided for segment reorganization as part of SAP's roadmap.

SAP has no standard functionality to reorganize company codes and plants. Changes on this level are defined at SAP as mergers and acquisitions. Such mergers and acquisition scenarios are usually handled by the SAP data migration and landscape transformation (DMLT) consultants and are custom specific projects using generic technical tools for copying data within or across tables. 

Summarizing my answer: for this custom-specific setup of segmentation using company codes, SAP has no reorganization functionality and is - at this point in time - not planning to provide one.

entry.title: Set severity level for Fault Exceptions for Proxy Interface.
entry.content:
HI Experts,

I have fault exceptions created for a Proxy to raise faults when there are errors in the incoming data.

Currently, all these faults are seen as 'Application Errors' in sxmb_moni transaction, Can this 'Overall Status' field in sxmb_moni can be configured ?. Instead of Error, can I show this as a 'Warning' with a Yellow triangle Icon ? Can this be set using the 'Severity' for fault Exceptions ?

Suggestions are highly appreciated.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Custom field in service entry sheet Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I spent so many hours on debug and looking for any valuable information - unfortunately with no result.

I have requirement to extend view which shows during service entry sheet creation using purchase order reference. After giving PO number and pressing enter we see view with PO items. And this is what I want to extend. I have to add another column to this table/view(CDS ?). 

I checked app documentation. There is information about CDS's which could be extended:

App Extensibility: Manage Service Entry Sheets - Lean Services | SAP Help Portal

but in my opinion its related with SES view extension. Seems its not my case.

View that I would like to extend is below. I looked for something like *_VH with no result.

Thank you for any help.

entry.title: Re: How to Enable zoom in and zoom out print preview ?
entry.content: I am getting the same when I click the PDF preview. Anyone found the issue?

entry.title: Sales Order price is updated from sales contract after approval instead of custom logic in pricing
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We are using SAP S/4HANA public cloud version and we have enabled custom business object to enable customized sales pricing in sales order. 

Following are the steps we have done and it is surprising to note that Sales Order price is updated from sales contract after approval instead of custom logic in pricing.

Step 1 : custom business logic for sales pricing for materials 

Step 2: Sales Contract creation 

Step 3: Sales Order with reference to sales contract 

Step 3 : Sales Order approval

Observation : Sales pricing  for material in Sales Order is getting updated from sales contract instead of customized logic for pricing. 

Sales Contract 

 

custom business logic 

Sales Order creation 

 

Sales Order approval

After sales Order approval 

sales order approval action is impacting our custom logic for sales price and the sales price for material in sales order is picking up from sales contract. 

Could you please advise on how to avoid the sales price copying from sales contract. 

Our requirement is to pull the sales price only from customized business object. 

Please advise. 

Thank you

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: idoc not updating multiple job to position relationships in the HRP1001 table
entry.content:
Dear All,

Below is my Query.

I am creating Job to position relationships in SAP HCM using inbound idocs . In my case there is multiple number of same job mapped to different positions. The IDOC status running successfully with status 53 however  it is creating only only the last line item in HRP1001  table. Below is the screenshot for your reference.

There are single job for multiple positions, Once I run the application its creates the inbound IDOCs, I am processing the IDOCS using BD87

Above are idoc records

Everything seems ok however HRP1001 is creating only the last position.As per my understanding it should create 3 line items for each job .

Please verify and suggest.

Thanks

Jay

entry.title: Re: Unit of Measure Conversion In “Manage Prices – Sales” App
entry.content:
Thanks for the great explanation.

but I did not find those two column Numerator and Denominator in my system. Is this authorization issue?

entry.content: Even I am also facing the same issue. Any solution ?

entry.title: Re: Internal to Excel with multiple sheets and formatting
entry.content:
I agree with Matt,

I've used ABAP2XLSX several times and it's great.

We also had a technical audit to all Z developments and ABAP2XLSX passed without any remarks.

entry.title: Re: Want to start journey with SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
Dear David_Chaviano

I have seen there is again opportunity for Women to start their SAP career in 2024 via Digital Skill Initiative. I read your blog. In that blog it is mentioned that "The self-study portion of the digital skills initiative is coming to an end." 

I have eagerly waited for your reply which you mentioned early in mail trail. Below are some point mentioned in your blog. For next step what should I need to do could you please elaborate more, as I want to start my Career in SAP TM with certification.

  • Application texts can be submitted here until November 4, 2024.
  • Your learning must be completed by December 1, 2024, and a screenshot of completion together with your SAP Community username must be submitted to digitalskills@sap.com by this day at the latest.
  • We will process your request and send you your redemption code by December 20, 2024 at the latest.
  • You must redeem your code in the training shop by December 31, 2024, but can schedule your exam for any date in the following 12 months  (December 31, 2025 at the latest.)

entry.title: Re: Audit trail to track old and new values on table, HAN...
entry.content: Out of curiosity, no offense intended by me, is it an answer made by Generative AI?

entry.title: HowTo: Enable Semantical Date Ranges in Fiori Elements Overview Pages
entry.content:
Semantical date ranges are a powerful feature that allows users to filter data using dynamic date values such as "Last 30 Days" or "Next Week". For List Report Pages, enabling this is straightforward by following SAP's official guide. However, enabling date ranges in Overview Pages requires a slightly different approach.

To activate semantical date ranges in Overview Pages, you need to modify the manifest.json. Unlike List Report Pages, you must configure the "sap.ovp" section and adjust the filterSettings.

Here’s the configuration you need to add:

{
  ...
  "sap.ovp": {
    ...
    "filterSettings": {
      "dateSettings": {
        "useDateRange": true
      }
    }
  }
}

 

This provides a more flexible and dynamic filtering experience, making it easier for users to access relevant data quickly.

Good luck with your implementation!

entry.title: How can I make catalog and tile names multi-language in SAP FIORI?
entry.content:
The purpose of this article is to explain how to implement multi-language support for the title information of a tile created in SAP Fiori. The goal is to ensure that when the user selects English, the tile's title is displayed in English, and similarly, when the user selects Turkish, the tile's title is displayed in Turkish. This allows for a seamless language experience in Fiori applications, providing localized content based on the user's language preference.

Step 1

The first step is to locate the DESCRIPTION ID on the catalog page (highlighted with a yellow marker).

Step 2

In the SAP screen, navigate to the following screen using the SE63 transaction code (pay attention to the Client). The screen that appears is as shown below. The “Short Texts” button should be clicked to open the “Object Type Selection” screen, and from the “Meta Objects”, double-click on the “TABL Tables (Meta)” option.

Step 3

The screen that will appear is as shown below. The “Object Name” field should be filled with the code "WDY_CONF_USERT2". In the “Source Language” field (the language you want to translate from), enter "English", and in the “Target Language” field (the language you want to translate to), enter "Turkish". You can proceed to the next step by clicking the “Edit” button.

 

Step 4

In the window that opens, enter the “Configuration ID” to find the appropriate “Description ID” (as shown in Step 1). After locating the relevant ID, double-click on the selected ID.

Step 5

In the window that opens, click on the **Execute** button.

Step 6

In the screen that opens, enter the catalog names in the desired language in the rows indicated by the “Green Line”. Then, save the changes by clicking the “Save” button.

Step 7

You should navigate to the SE38 screen and clear the cache. The steps are as follows:

Fill the Program field with the code "/UI2/INVALIDATE_CLIENT_CACHES" and execute it using the green button.

The Program field should be filled with the code "/UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES" and executed using the green button.

Step 8

The processes have been completed. You can go to the screen where you want to perform the operation and test it.

entry.title: REST -> Hana / Data Provisioning Adapter deployment issue, help with Maven or configuration needed
entry.content:
Hello community, 

I am trying to develope a REST Webservice Adapter for a customer. 

I have used the "WebserviceAdapter"open source code from here: https://github.com/SAP-archive/hana-native-adapters as a scaffold and followed this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/provisioning-data-from-web-services-with-custom-adapter-using-sap-hana-sdi/ba-p/13377052.

The REST service requires a header thats encrypted so I am using the bouncycastle package to develope that. 

I have added the package manually to the build path and added the package to the "Import-Package" statement in the manifest.mf file. 

Based on the SAP Dokumentation I need to export the project using "Export" -> "Deployable plug-ins and fragments" I do not get any errors, however when deploying the .jar in the enviroment I get the following error: 

"Unresolved requirement: Import-Package: org.bouncycastle; version="1.78.0"

I have been struggling with this for a while and can not figure out what the issue is. 

I would love to change the project into a maven project. In the open source project I can see that one adapter is a maven project: jdbcadapter however the pom.xml file inherits its configuration from a pom.xml file that is not available. 

I do not know how to fix the issue nor how to configure the pom.xml to build a "Deployable plug-ins and fragments" through maven. Does anyone know if I can use maven and create a "normal" runable jar if that would be deployable as well? 

Any help would be much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: how to hide the confirmation page on mobile browser
entry.content:
I found two related questions, but I can't read more because I don't have an account.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2607850

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2790188

entry.title: How can I make catalog and tile names multi-language in SAP FIORI?
entry.content:
How can I make catalog and tile names multi-language in SAP FIORI?

 

The purpose of this article is to explain how to implement multi-language support for the title information of a tile created in SAP Fiori. The goal is to ensure that when the user selects English, the tile's title is displayed in English, and similarly, when the user selects Turkish, the tile's title is displayed in Turkish. This allows for a seamless language experience in Fiori applications, providing localized content based on the user's language preference.

Step 1

The first step is to locate the DESCRIPTION ID on the catalog page (highlighted with a yellow marker).

Step 2

In the SAP screen, navigate to the following screen using the SE63 transaction code (pay attention to the Client). The screen that appears is as shown below. The “Short Texts” button should be clicked to open the “Object Type Selection” screen, and from the “Meta Objects”, double-click on the “TABL Tables (Meta)” option.

Step 3

The screen that will appear is as shown below. The “Object Name” field should be filled with the code "WDY_CONF_USERT2". In the “Source Language” field (the language you want to translate from), enter "English", and in the “Target Language” field (the language you want to translate to), enter "Turkish". You can proceed to the next step by clicking the “Edit” button.

 

Step 4

In the window that opens, enter the “Configuration ID” to find the appropriate “Description ID” (as shown in Step 1). After locating the relevant ID, double-click on the selected ID.

 

 

Step 5

In the window that opens, click on the **Execute** button.

Step 6

In the screen that opens, enter the catalog names in the desired language in the rows indicated by the “Green Line”. Then, save the changes by clicking the “Save” button.

Step 7

You should navigate to the SE38 screen and clear the cache. The steps are as follows:

Fill the Program field with the code "/UI2/INVALIDATE_CLIENT_CACHES" and execute it using the green button.

The Program field should be filled with the code "/UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES" and executed using the green button.

 

Step 8

The processes have been completed. You can go to the screen where you want to perform the operation and test it.

entry.title: Re: Audit trail to track old and new values on table, HAN...
entry.content: Please remember our Rules of Engagement indicate that if GenAI is used it needs to be declared with a user label or in the text that it was "GenAI Assisted Content"

entry.title: 診断エージェントに対するCA署名済み証明書を利用したSSLセットアップについて
entry.content:
SAPグローバルサポート御中

お世話になっております。

掲題について、下記のノートよりSSLサーバ証明書についてSolmanが自己署名証明書を利用する場合はサーバ認証が行われないことを確認済みです。

セキュリティ要件からサーバ認証を行うため、プライベートCAによる署名済み証明書を利用する予定です。

診断エージェント側に対する信頼済みCA一覧の更新は、Solmanのエージェント管理アプリケーションから実施可能な認識です。

顧客側にはDAA側のキーストアの仕様について説明する必要があるため、キーストアファイル名や格納場所についてご教示ください。

＜参考ノート＞

https://me.sap.com/notes/2574394


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 診断エージェントに対するCA署名済み証明書を利用したSSLセットアップについて
entry.content:
ご担当者　様

いつもお世話になっております。

エージェント管理アプリケーションから ｢信頼済みCA一覧の更新｣をクリックすることより、下記のパスでDAA側のキーストア.CertificatesKeyStoreファイルが更新されます。

/usr/sap/<SID_AGT>/SMDA<Instance_Number>/SMDAgent/configuration/security/.CertificatesKeyStore

宜しくお願いいたします。

SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: CIC Support Webcasts
entry.content: Thanks Claire, well explained 🙂

entry.title: Re: GRDC-Create forms for multiple consolidation units
entry.content: Hello, your question has been forwarded to the experts. We hope to provide an answer soon. Kind regards

entry.title: SAP MDG Parallel Change Requests for Material Enrichment Success Stories
entry.content:
Dear fellow SAP MDG specialists, 

Has anyone ever implemented the Parallel MDG Change Request Concept (not to be confused with parallel Workflow Work Items within the same CR!) in MDG for material master (or other objects) enrichment as part of a new record creation process? If so, can you please share your experience? The capability is well documented by SAP but I have not personally seen it implemented yet. 

Business scenario: A company follows a guiding principle in a material master creation process where the knowledge holders need to enrich the data with the correct attribute values in MDG CRs. Some of these business roles / workflow agents are plant-specific (e.g. Planning Data Expert). The company's material master creation solution today consists of several follow-up CRs, some with parallel workflow items (grouped by functional domains within a material). Today the company creates and extends materials for one plant at a time (in a single record creation scenario). However, in the future they would like to be able to create and enrich materials for multiple plants in a single process iteration. The challenge is that because they have plant specific roles, a large number of workflow agents would need to be involved in the enrichment process. One of the options the company is considering is using parallel MDG CRs (one per plant) during the material enrichment process. In this case an initial CR with a list of plants would trigger parallel follow-up CRs for plant-specific enrichment. 

Any insights or alternatives would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Integration track sessions
entry.content:
We are days away from the first session of this year's Devtoberfest, our annual virtual celebration dedicated to the SAP developer community. There is a lot going on in the integration space, and as part of Devtoberfest 2024 we will have sessions covering the following topics: APIs and Events, Tools for integration developers, open source and more.

As you might be aware, every track is assigned a unique day across the four weeks that Devtoberfest takes place and Wednesdays will be all about integration.

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

You can find all the different tracks/sessions that are part of Devtoberfest here. You can filter all events by selecting the topic (label) you are interested in, e.g. integration 😉.

Let me quickly share all the sessions that we will have as part of the integration track:

25th of September:

  • 09:30 CET - 🔴FlashPipe - Your CI/CD Companion for SAP Integration Suite
  • 11:00 CET - 🔴Speed up your SAP Cloud Integration Development with CPI-Helper
  • 13:00 CET - 🔴Automate your SAP Cloud Integration governance with CPILint

2nd of October:

  • 09:30 CET - 🔴Developing modern terminal-based tools for SAP Cloud Integration
  • 11:00 CET - 🔴Navigating EDA Complexity: SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Mesh and AEM Event Portal Solutions
  • 15:00 CET - 🔴CloudEvents for interoperable event systems

16th of October:

  • 09:30 CET - 🔴🔴 Harnessing S/4HANA Events in SAP Build
  • 11:00 CET - 🔴Filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and in AEM
  • 15:00 CET - 🔴Discover SAP Event Sources for Advanced Event Mesh
  • 16:30 CET - 🔴Use SAP Event Broker for SAP cloud applications to consume business events in a CAP application

25th of October:

  • 09:30:00 CET - 🔴Improve Operational Efficiency using AI-enabled API Anomaly Detection
  • 15:00 CET - 🔴Enterprise Automation with SAP: Latest Enhancements

Note: If you click on any of the times of the sessions above, you will download the calendar invite for the session.

So go ahead and RSVP to the different sessions you are interested in. Also, make sure to download the calendar invites so that you don't miss the session and you can get a chance to ask the speaker any questions that you might have about the topic.

Happy learning this Devtoberfest 2024!

entry.title: Why our company colors are always messed up after each release?
entry.content:
After every release, our company layout of the SF suite are all messed up.

We have a blue background and every time we have disappearing titles or buttons because their color is changed from white (which is visible on blue) to black (which becomes invisible).

We do a comprehensive check every time on Test but even if we find the issues and open a ticket, the solution never comes before the release is in Production and we have to live for months with the wrong layouts.

Isn't it possible to have a general check done by you BEFORE you release your upgrades? It's something you should take care of, not us!

SAP SuccessFactors Platform UI theme designer

entry.title: MSSQL Setup on MAC using backpac dump from Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

The need to debug and play with the real data is evident, what if you don't have enough data on your local MAC to check all the scenarios? Some developers would surely like to get the data from SAP Commerce Cloud infrastructure and use it locally. If you are one such developer, continue reading.

Pre-requisites

1. Docker must be installed
2. have a db dump .bacpac file

Docker setup

First, we need to create a docker image for the MSSQL, and then we will start the server on Docker.

If you don't have Docker, get it downloaded and setup from here.
Start Docker, and run the following commands (with your changes) on Terminal.
This pulls the latest image for the MSSQL server:

 

 

 

 

docker pull mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest

 

 

 

 

Now run the following command to create and run your new docker container.

 

 

 

 

docker run -e "ACCEPT_EULA=Y" -e "MSSQL_USER=SA" -e "MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD=yourSecretPassword123" -p 1433:1433 --name mssql --hostname mssql --platform linux/amd64 -d mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest

 

 

 

 

Here you can see that we are using the following:
1. user = SA
2. password = yourSecretPassword123 (use your own)
3. port = 1433 
4. server name = mssql
5. hostname = mssql
6. platform = linux/amd64

Now, I've used "mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server:2022-latest" to get the latest image and not used "mcr.microsoft.com/azure-sql-edge:latest" as I had some issues with azure-sql-edge version while creating the database using bacpac file.

After this command, you should have a new container running, something like this:

Installing Azure Data Studio

Instead of running a bunch of commands from the Terminal, we will mostly be using the Azure Data Studio for creating our database out of the db dump file.
Azure Data Studio is a lightweight, cross-platform data management and development tool with connectivity to popular cloud and on-premises databases. Azure Data Studio supports Windows, macOS, and Linux, with immediate capability to connect to Azure SQL and SQL Server.
Here is the setup:

1. Install Azure Data Studio: Download here
On the page scroll down and make sure to download the correct version for your MAC (based on the chip)






2. Run Azure Data Studio: Once downloaded and installed, run the Azure Data Studio, you should see the following screen:



3. Make the connection with MSSQL server running in Docker:
Click on New -> New Connection, and specify the connection details on the dialog:


Click on "Enable trust..." if a dialog popup appears.


Now the left pane should show the connection, you should also be able to see a screen similar to this:



4. Install extensions for Azure Data Studio: 
Now, let us install a few more extensions to Azure Data Studio so that we can import the bacpac file.
Install the extensions listed in the image below, not all of these are required for importing the bacpac file, but are really helpful in many other admin functions, make sure to install DACPAC extension, this extension is required for working with bacpac files.

5. Import the dump file:
Now, before you import the dump file, make sure that the virtual disk space allocated to Docker is more than the DB Size that you are importing. For instance, if your DB size is 10GB, make sure you allocate at least 20GB to Docker, remember that there are other containers as well, in that case you might have to increase the virtual disk space accordingly.

Here is how to increase the disk space for Docker, in my case I was dealing with a 55GB database, so I increased the Visrual disk space to 120GB. This will restart your Docker, make sure that the MSSQL server is running after Docker restarts.



Restart Azure Data Studio, on the left panel under Databases, right click and select "Data-tier Application Wizard"

Select Create a database from a .bacpac file

Provide .bacpac file location and enter any database name for your liking, then follow the wizard.

This process will start the task of creating the database, when the task is finished you will be able to see your new database under the databases, similar to the image below:

Modify local.properties to connect to this DB

After the database is available in Azure Data Studio, and the import is finished, connect to the new DB by changing the database properties in the local.properties file:

 

db.url=jdbc:sqlserver://localhost:1433;databaseName=${db.name};responseBuffering=adaptive;loginTimeout=10;disableStatementPooling=false;statementPoolingCacheSize=1000
db.driver=com.microsoft.sqlserver.jdbc.SQLServerDriver
db.username=sa
db.password=yourPassword123
db.name=mydb
db.tableprefix=

 

Conclusion

There are only a few resources available online which allows you to import bacpac file to your own local setup on a MAC device. I hope this helps you with your development struggles, all the best.

Additional Perks: You could use Azure Data Studio to run queries on the connected databases. Enjoy.

entry.title: Material type and material group restrictions in S4 HANA
entry.content:
Restricting material types and material groups in SAP S4 HANA 

In S4 HANA Procurement, material types and material groups are restricted using authorization objects M_MATE_MAR (Material Master: Material Types) and M_MATE_WGR (Material Master: Material Groups). Both these objects have a common field value called BEGRU (Authorization group).

Material types and material groups cannot be directly maintained in BEGRU. First, use the Tcode SE54 to create authorization groups. The authorization groups must then be mapped to material types and groups using the T-codes OMS2 and OMSF. Then these authorization groups should be maintained in a role.

Note: Material types with no authorization group will skip the M_MATE_MAR authority check. material groups with no authorization group will skip M_MATE_WGR. Hence, authorization groups should be maintained against all the material types (allowed and restricted) and material groups (allowed and restricted) to make the access restrictions work.

 

Authorization object

Description

Field1

Description

Field2

Description

M_MATE_MAR

(Material Master: Material Types)

Actvt

Activity

BEGRU

Authorization group

M_MATE_WGR

(Material Master: Material Groups)

Actvt

Activity

BEGRU

Authorization group

Let us look at an example of restricting users' access to specific material types and groups while using the T-codes MM01, MM02, and MM03.

Material types allowed for users – Goods. All the other material types other than Goods should be restricted for users.

Material groups allowed for users - Finance Services. All the other material groups other than Finance Services should be restricted for users.

Two sets of authorization groups should be created in SE54 for the case described above.

Z001 for allowed material types.

Z002 for restricted material types.

Z003 for allowed material groups.

Z004 for restricted material groups.

Steps:

  • Create authorization groups for material types and material groups using the Tcode SE54.

  • Map authorization groups with the material types using the Tcode OMS2 (maintain the authorization group Z001 against the allowed material types (Goods) and maintain the authorization group Z002 against all the restricted material types.)

  • Map authorization groups with the material groups using the T-code OMSF (maintain the authorization group Z003 against all the allowed material groups (Finance Services) and maintain the authorization group Z004 against all the restricted material groups.)

  • Maintain the authorization groups in the authorization objects (M_MATE_MAR and M_MATE_WGR) in PFCG.

entry.title: Re: API 「G/L Account Line Items - Read」 get Open document
entry.content: Thanks for your question. It has been forwarded again to the experts.

entry.title: Re: Hide the "post on account " tab in post outgoing paym...
entry.content: You need to look for options within SAPUI5 flexibility to find out how to hide or remove tabs in a Fiori application. I'm not an expert in that area, sorry.

entry.title: Re: TMS - Transport SAP Cloud Integration (CI/CPI) Content with Transport Management Service (TMS) a
entry.content:
@Archana_Gaddam 

Destination 1 - created in source CI account point to Content Agent service service "standard". This is required by Cloud Integration service to request Content Agent to start the export by sharing the metadata for export request and start the async transport operation. 
Destination 2 - created in source CI account point to Cloud Integration (Process Integration Runtime) "it-rt" service and "api" plan. This is required by Content Agent service to call back the CPI service pulling the required integration package to create a bundle and push to Cloud Transport Management. 

Alternatively you can also try to use Content Agent UI to transport Integration Packages. If you chose this option then Destination 1 need not be created anymore. Here are two blogs that describe the using Content Agent Service UI. 
New User Interface for Content Agent Service
How to use SAP Content Agent Service (Video)

entry.title: Re: Use of multiple tax conditions in same pricing procedure.

do you have an experience with down payment processing with multiple tax conditions?

 

Thank you.

Martina

entry.title: Re: How to create new fields for journal entries mass upload in public cloud?
entry.content: Since the 'Upload General Journal Entries' is an enhancement of the 'Post General Journal Entries' app, both share the same Validation and Substitution Rules.
